title: ononis repens subsp. australis
intro: Ononis repens subsp. australis, also known as the creeping restharrow, is a subspecies of the Ononis repens plant, which is a type of perennial herb native to New Zealand. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria, making it a potential candidate for the development of antimicrobial agents. The creeping restharrow has been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the creeping restharrow include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and other remedies for various health benefits.
